target.title: Fix skill config requirements, spawn allowlists, chat stop lifecycle, and systemd status
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #79488 by normalizing object-shaped `metadata.openclaw.requires.config` entries through their `path` field so skill status/info no longer prints `[object Object]` or blocks valid documented requirements.
- Fixes #79490 by honoring `OPENCLAW_SPAWN_ALLOWLIST` / `SPAWN_ALLOWLIST` as fallback subagent target allowlists when config does not provide one.
- Fixes #79525 by mapping backend agent run ids back to originating WebChat client runs so context-overflow/error completions clear the active run and unblock Stop / queued `/new` lifecycle state.
- Fixes #79515 by preferring `systemctl is-active` when `systemctl show` reports a stale failed state for the Gateway user service.
- Rebased onto current `upstream/main` so the production dependency audit includes the `fast-uri` pin already present on main.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Four user-facing regressions: skill config requirements rendered object metadata as `[object Object]`, subagent spawn env allowlists were ignored, WebChat run state could stay active after backend error/context-overflow completion, and `openclaw status` could report a systemd Gateway service failed even when `systemctl is-active` reported active.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Windows checkout at `705723b9d0` after rebasing onto `upstream/main`; Node v22.15.0 for repo test wrappers; WSL Ubuntu has systemd installed but no user bus and no Linux Node, so it could not provide honest live user-systemd proof on this host.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the touched skill/subagent/chat/frontmatter tests in one grouped repo invocation, ran the focused systemd stale-state regression, ran production dependency audit, and ran the changed gate from the rebased branch.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output summary from the final local run:
```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillstatus.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.allowlist.test.ts src/agents/subagent-target-policy.test.ts src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.test.ts src/shared/frontmatter.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 84.38s
unit-fast: 17 passed
agents: 30 passed
gateway: 74 passed

OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/daemon/systemd.test.ts -t "prefers is-active" -- --reporter=verbose
✓ systemd runtime parsing > prefers is-active when show reports a stale failed state
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed | 60 skipped (61)

node scripts/pre-commit/pnpm-audit-prod.mjs --audit-level=high
No high or higher advisories found for production dependencies.
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Object-shaped skill requirements normalize correctly, `SPAWN_ALLOWLIST` fallback allowlists are respected, chat final/error lifecycle maps the backend run id to the client run, and the systemd parser prefers active `is-active` output over stale failed `show` state. The dependency-audit failure from the old branch base is gone after rebase.
- **What was not tested:** A live Linux user-systemd `openclaw status` run could not be executed on this Windows host because WSL reports `systemctl --user` bus unavailable and has no Linux Node/pnpm. The full `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts` file also has unrelated Windows-only failures around `process.geteuid` and quoted Windows `EnvironmentFile` paths; the new stale-state regression passed in isolation.

## Verification
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/tools/creating-skills.md docs/tools/skills.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/acp-spawn.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.allowlist.test.ts src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillstatus.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/agents/subagent-target-policy.test.ts src/agents/subagent-target-policy.ts src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.ts src/daemon/systemd.test.ts src/daemon/systemd.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/shared/frontmatter.test.ts src/shared/frontmatter.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillstatus.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.allowlist.test.ts src/agents/subagent-target-policy.test.ts src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.test.ts src/shared/frontmatter.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/daemon/systemd.test.ts -t "prefers is-active" -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/pre-commit/pnpm-audit-prod.mjs --audit-level=high`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`

Note: this shell's default Node is v22.15.0, so pnpm emitted the repo engine warning (`>=22.16.0` required); the commands above completed with that warning.

target.title: test(browser): cover action input request shapes
target.body:
Refs #83877

## Summary
- Add small, focused CLI registrar tests for browser action-input request shapes that current main still does not assert.
- Cover representative happy paths for click, navigate, resize, and fill while leaving runtime browser behavior unchanged.
- Keep the scope intentionally smaller than the earlier closed broad coverage PR by extending only existing tests.

## Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.element.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.files-downloads.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.element.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.element.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** #83877 reports that browser action-input CLI command request-body wiring and validation could regress silently because most command registrars lacked CLI-layer coverage.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node/pnpm dependencies installed in this worktree. In addition to targeted Vitest, I ran the real browser CLI source registrar directly with `node --import tsx` and `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_LAZY_SUBCOMMANDS=1`, without a test framework, to verify the action-input commands are present and the CLI validation path fires before any gateway request.

**Exact steps or command run after fix:**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.element.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.files-downloads.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.element.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.element.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.test.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.test.ts
git diff --check
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { Command } from "commander";
import { registerBrowserCli } from "./extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.ts";
import { defaultRuntime } from "./extensions/browser/src/cli/core-api.ts";
process.env.OPENCLAW_DISABLE_LAZY_SUBCOMMANDS = "1";
// Registers the real source CLI, checks action-input help, then runs blank-ref validation.
EOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Targeted Vitest passed 4 files / 20 tests in the browser extension shard. The new tests assert real Commander parsing for `browser click`, `browser navigate`, `browser resize`, and `browser fill`, then verify the outgoing `/act` or `/navigate` request body, profile query, target id, timeout, and resize runner parameters.

Copied live output from the direct source-registrar proof:
```text
PASS: click help
  command: browser click --help
  tokens: Click an element by ref, --target-id, --double
PASS: navigate help
  command: browser navigate --help
  tokens: Navigate the current tab to a URL, --target-id
PASS: fill help
  command: browser fill --help
  tokens: Fill a form with JSON field descriptors, --fields, --fields-file
PASS: resize help
  command: browser resize --help
  tokens: Resize the viewport, --target-id
PASS: blank click ref validation
  command: browser click "   "
  runtimeExit: 1
  runtimeErrors: ref is required
```


**Observed result after fix:** Browser action-input tests now cover representative happy-path request shapes in addition to the validation-focused tests that were already on main. The direct source-registrar proof confirms the real browser CLI exposes the action-input commands and rejects a blank click ref locally before dispatch. The patch is test-only and touches no production browser CLI, gateway, or runtime behavior.

**What was not tested:** A live browser server or gateway was not started because the patch only adds CLI registrar tests and does not change browser runtime behavior.

target.title: docs(notion): make allowlist examples direct
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the bundled Notion skill documented shell-local `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)` setup and multi-line `curl` examples that are hostile to strict exec allowlists.
- Why it matters: users running `security=allowlist` with `ask=off` can follow the bundled docs and still hit `exec denied: allowlist miss`, even after allowlisting the underlying `curl` binary.
- What changed: the skill now points setup at `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` / `NOTION_API_KEY`, explains the strict-allowlist pitfall, and gives a file-backed `python3 notion_request.py ...` helper flow for common operations.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no exec-approval policy, Notion API behavior, runtime code, or bundled helper file was added; this is a docs-only repair for the bundled skill guidance.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #59235
- Related #59254, #59870
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: The bundled `skills/notion/SKILL.md` no longer teaches the allowlist-hostile `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)` / shell-wrapped multi-line `curl` workflow. It now documents `NOTION_API_KEY` setup and direct `python3 notion_request.py ...` helper invocations for strict allowlist setups.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Windows, branch `feat/notion-allowlist-friendly-docs`, inspecting the updated bundled Notion skill file after the patch and running the repo docs/format gates.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
Select-String -LiteralPath skills\notion\SKILL.md -Pattern "Strict exec allowlist note|Allowlist-friendly helper|python3 notion_request|NOTION_API_KEY|curl" -Context 0,2
rg "curl -X|NOTION_KEY=|\$\(cat|; curl|\\\s*$" skills/notion/SKILL.md
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 skills/notion/SKILL.md
git diff --check
pnpm docs:list
pnpm check:changed
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live terminal output from the patched checkout:

```text
skills\notion\SKILL.md:20:3. Configure the skill with `NOTION_API_KEY`. OpenClaw injects `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` into `process.env.NOTION_API_KEY` for the agent turn because this skill declares `metadata.openclaw.primaryEnv`.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:39:All requests need the `Authorization`, `Notion-Version`, and usually `Content-Type` headers. Use `NOTION_API_KEY`; do not create a shell-local alias such as `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)`.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:41:Strict exec allowlist note: avoid examples shaped like `NOTION_KEY=...; curl ...`, command substitution, shell chaining, and line continuations. In `security=allowlist` with `ask=off`, those forms can be denied as an allowlist miss even when the underlying `curl` binary is allowlisted. Prefer a direct executable invocation with secrets already supplied in the environment, or a small file-backed helper run through an allowlisted interpreter.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:45:### Allowlist-friendly helper
skills\notion\SKILL.md:47:For strict allowlist setups, create a helper file once and run it directly with an allowlisted `python3`. The helper reads `NOTION_API_KEY` from the environment and reads request bodies from files, so command lines do not need shell variable assignment, command substitution, semicolon chaining, or continuation characters.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:64:api_key = os.environ["NOTION_API_KEY"]
skills\notion\SKILL.md:100:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/search search.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:106:python3 notion_request.py GET /v1/pages/{page_id}
skills\notion\SKILL.md:112:python3 notion_request.py GET /v1/blocks/{page_id}/children
skills\notion\SKILL.md:130:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/pages create-page.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:145:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/data_sources/{data_source_id}/query query-data-source.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:165:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/data_sources create-data-source.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:177:python3 notion_request.py PATCH /v1/pages/{page_id} update-page.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:197:python3 notion_request.py PATCH /v1/blocks/{page_id}/children append-blocks.json

rg "curl -X|NOTION_KEY=|\$\(cat|; curl|\\\s*$" skills/notion/SKILL.md
All requests need the `Authorization`, `Notion-Version`, and usually `Content-Type` headers. Use `NOTION_API_KEY`; do not create a shell-local alias such as `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)`.
Strict exec allowlist note: avoid examples shaped like `NOTION_KEY=...; curl ...`, command substitution, shell chaining, and line continuations. In `security=allowlist` with `ask=off`, those forms can be denied as an allowlist miss even when the underlying `curl` binary is allowlisted. Prefer a direct executable invocation with secrets already supplied in the environment, or a small file-backed helper run through an allowlisted interpreter.
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched Notion skill presents the allowlist-safe `NOTION_API_KEY` setup and direct helper-command examples for every common operation. The old executable examples no longer include multi-line `curl -X ...`, `NOTION_KEY=...; curl ...`, or command-substitution setup; the remaining `curl`/`NOTION_KEY` mentions are warnings describing what to avoid.
- What was not tested: No live Notion API request was sent because that would require a real Notion workspace/integration secret. The changed behavior is documentation guidance and command shape, so validation focused on the bundled skill content and docs checks.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, `skills/notion/SKILL.md` told users to run `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ~/.config/notion/api_key)` and then use multi-line `curl` commands with `$NOTION_KEY`, which matches the allowlist-miss shape reported in #59235.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Notion skill docs predated the stricter allowlist guidance and used shell-local variable assignment, command substitution, and multi-line `curl` examples even though strict allowlist parsing can reject shell control/expansion shapes before reaching the underlying executable.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The skill doc did not call out strict exec allowlist constraints or prefer a direct executable/helper shape for secure configurations.
- Contributing context (if known): Prior fix attempts (#59254 and #59870) were closed unmerged, and the current bundled skill still contained the reported pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `skills/notion/SKILL.md`
- Scenario the test should lock in: The Notion skill should not recommend shell-substitution/chained `curl` examples for strict allowlist users.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: This is a docs-only issue; the existing exec-approval tests already cover why command substitution, shell chaining, and line continuations are allowlist-hostile.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts` covers the parser constraints; this PR updates the skill docs to respect them.
- If no new test is added, why not: No runtime behavior changed; markdown formatting and docs listing are the relevant local gates.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users reading the bundled Notion skill now get allowlist-friendly setup and request examples instead of a shell-wrapped `curl` workflow that can fail under strict exec policies.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Notion skill docs -> NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...) + multi-line curl -> strict allowlist miss risk

After:
Notion skill docs -> NOTION_API_KEY + file-backed python3 helper -> direct allowlist-friendly command shape
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No runtime change`; docs now point at the existing `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` / `NOTION_API_KEY` secret path instead of suggesting a separate shell-local alias.
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No runtime change`; docs recommend direct helper invocation instead of shell wrappers.
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node/pnpm workspace checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): bundled Notion skill docs
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A; no Notion secret used

### Steps

1. Inspect the patched Notion skill content for `NOTION_API_KEY`, strict allowlist guidance, and direct helper invocations.
2. Search for remaining bad executable examples (`curl -X`, `NOTION_KEY=`, `$(`, `; curl`, line-continuation command endings).
3. Run docs/format gates.

### Expected

- The skill docs guide strict allowlist users toward direct executable/helper commands and avoid the old shell-wrapped `curl` examples.

### Actual

- The patched skill includes the helper flow and the bad patterns only remain inside the explanatory “avoid this” note.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: inspected the patched skill output, confirmed helper commands are present for search/page/block/data-source operations, confirmed `oxfmt`, `git diff --check`, `docs:list`, and `check:changed` pass.
- Edge cases checked: confirmed the old bad command shapes are not still presented as runnable examples; remaining `curl`/`NOTION_KEY` text is warning-only.
- What I did **not** verify: live Notion API auth/request behavior with a real workspace, because this PR changes docs only and no Notion secret was used.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No runtime config change`; docs now recommend the existing `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` path.
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users may copy the helper without setting `NOTION_API_KEY` in their environment/config first.
  - Mitigation: Setup now explicitly documents `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` and says OpenClaw injects it as `NOTION_API_KEY`.

target.title: fix(discord): log silent reply drops; allow archives in host-read media
target.body:
## Summary

Two related fixes that surfaced while debugging an operator's Discord bot that
appeared to "stop replying" mid-conversation.

- **`fix(discord)`** — Reply payloads were being silently dropped at two
  sanitization gates: `sanitizeDiscordFrontChannelReplyPayloads` (when no
  sendable content remained after sanitization) and the zero-payloads
  early-return inside `deliverDiscordFrontChannelReplyPayloads`. Neither
  emitted any log entry, so the gateway log had nothing to explain why an
  agent's generated reply never reached the channel — symptoms looked like
  a frozen channel session. Add `warn`-level logs in the new
  `discord/reply-safety` and `discord/reply-delivery` subsystems including
  the originating `sessionKey`/`target` and the input vs. sanitized lengths.
  No behavior change beyond log lines.

- **`feat(media)`** — `HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES` rejected
  `application/zip` (and other common archive MIMEs), so any agent flow that
  built a `.zip` artifact and tried to deliver it via a channel hit
  `"Host-local media sends only allow buffer-verified images, audio, video,
  PDF, and Office documents (got application/zip)."` and the delivery got
  stuck in the queue indefinitely. Widen the allowlist to also accept zip /
  gzip / tar / 7z. Buffer-verification (file-type magic-byte sniffing) is
  unchanged: a file claimed to be a zip must still actually have a PK
  signature to pass — spoofing protection is preserved.

## Verification

- [x] `pnpm test src/media/web-media.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts` — 51 passed (39 + 12).
- [x] `pnpm build` — clean.
- [x] Built dist deployed to a local managed gateway (`openclaw gateway restart`); `gateway call health` returned `ok=true`, `eventLoop.degraded=false`, 12 plugins loaded, no errors.
- [x] End-to-end reply test via `openclaw agent` (no `--deliver`) — full path CLI → gateway → agent → model returned `PONG` from claude-opus-4-7, `stopReason: stop`.
- [ ] Live discord delivery proof — operator will validate by sending a fresh message in the affected channel after merge.

## Notes

- The third silent-return site flagged during recon
  (`outbound-adapter.ts:71-86` in `maybeSendDiscordWebhookText`) is an
  intentional fallback path that cascades to the regular `sendMessageDiscord`
  send when no webhook binding applies — not actually a drop. Left untouched
  to avoid log noise.
- Archive types deliberately do **not** include `application/octet-stream`
  or other un-sniffable MIMEs; an operator who needs truly opaque uploads
  should opt in via a separate config flag (out of scope here).

target.title: docs: document markdown and shared helpers
target.body:
## Summary

- Document markdown render marker, link, and option contracts used by `renderMarkdownWithMarkers`.
- Document inline-code scanner state and code-span lookup invariants used by streaming chunk/code awareness.
- Document markdown IR style/link/table contracts, parse options, slicing, and table-aware parse metadata.
- Document markdown chunking/table conversion contracts, including span rebasing and block-mode behavior.
- Document shared balanced JSON extraction and provider schema keyword stripping contracts.
- Document assistant/provider error parsing and UI formatting contracts.
- Document shared lazy loader, message content block visitor, and regexp escaping utility contracts.
- Document shared lazy runtime, safe import specifier, and reserved gateway method scope contracts.
- Document shared node matching and default node resolution contracts.
- Document shared device auth entry/store and adapter contracts.
- Document shared final-tag parser match and attribute-scanning contracts.
- Document shared custom command normalization and validation contracts.
- Document shared scoped expiring id cache method contracts.
- Document shared store writer task and FIFO queue field contracts.
- Document shared requirements metadata and diagnostics field contracts.
- Document shared runtime requirement field contracts for eligibility checks.
- Document shared thread binding lifecycle timestamp and timeout override contracts.
- Document shared node list and pairing payload field contracts.
- Document shared usage entry, aggregate, and result payload field contracts.
- Document shared usage timeseries point and series payload field contracts.
- Document shared entry metadata requirement evaluator input/result field contracts.
- Document channel mention gating fact, policy, and decision field contracts.
- Document channel command gating authorizer and control-command field contracts.
- Document channel thread binding spawn policy field contracts.
- Document channel target parser and mention-pattern policy helper contracts.
- Document channel ack reaction gate and cleanup helper contracts.
- Document direct-DM pre-crypto guard policy field contracts.
- Document channel route projection and native command session target helper contracts.
- Document command/inbound conversation resolution contracts and fallback semantics.
- Document inbound event context builder, supplemental filter, and command-turn helper contracts.
- Document channel reply pipeline, history window, and bot-loop guard helper contracts.
- Document message receipt, durable-send intent, and receipt id ordering contracts.
- Document live preview adapter and channel message capability proof contracts.
- Document durable message send/state and receive acknowledgement contracts.
- Document message outbound bridge result/adapter fields, rendered batch plan items, receipt id precedence, poll kind normalization, and rich payload kind inference.
- Document durable ingress queue and durable inbound receive journal method contracts, claim-token guards, race cleanup, and bounded pruning.
- Document message receive context method contracts and durable final requirement option semantics.
- Document inbound reply compatibility aliases, record/dispatch bridge params, and durable fallback ownership while removing a stale file-header overview.
- Document channel turn delivery fields, adapter hooks, durable fallback ownership, thread override semantics, and unknown-send queue policy.
- Document channel turn record/history options, assembled/prepared turn fields, lifecycle log fields, adapter methods, and runner params.
- Document SDK channel config write helpers and scoped/top-level/hybrid account config semantics.
- Document channel account snapshot projection, config presence detection, URL credential redaction, and persisted-auth metadata caching.
- Document channel match metadata, direct/parent/wildcard precedence, nested allowlist decisions, and allowlist candidate matching.
- Document direct-DM allow-from merging, access-group parsing, sender allow checks, and legacy authorizer bridge behavior.
- Document inbound debounce decisions and draft stream loop throttle/retry behavior.
- Document reply prefix context/options and typing-start guard trip/error semantics.
- Clarify the shared link/style close-stack ordering invariant without adding file headers or import-adjacent comments.

- Document thread-binding message labels and conversation label id-disambiguation helpers.

- Document sender identity validation, shared channel logging helpers, and safe inbound session metadata recording.

- Document webhook numeric option normalization, memory guard APIs, in-flight release behavior, and configured account id normalization.

- Document gateway session payload types and silent-reply policy classification/override contracts.

- Document extension-shared passive status, lifecycle, config issue, secret-ref, package version, and ambient proxy helpers.

- Document status-helper runtime lifecycle, computed account snapshot, and dependent credential issue contracts.

- Document migration item, config patch, apply, and redaction helper contracts while removing a stale file-top overview.

- Document QA live-transport scenario ids, selection, coverage, and missing-standard helpers.

- Document browser host-inspection facade helpers and plugin config runtime fallback behavior.

- Document provider model shared normalization and replay-family helper contracts while removing a stale file-top overview.

- Document direct command-status runtime params and unauthorized reasoning-state privacy guard.

- Document assistant text signature/extraction options and text segment joining semantics.

- Document scoped bearer-auth retry, OAuth form/PKCE helper, and shared chunk resolver contracts.

- Document account fallback and optional channel setup helper contracts.

- Document persistent and claimable dedupe helper contracts.

- Document provider tool compatibility normalization and inspection helper contracts.

- Document allowlist config edit helper contracts.

- Document provider onboarding preset and model merge helper contracts.

- Document provider catalog shared helper contracts.

- Document approval reaction runtime helper contracts.

- Document safe record helper contracts.

- Document browser maintenance cleanup helper contracts.

- Document plugin SDK runtime helper contracts for request-scoped subagent errors and cached lazy values, while removing a stale file-top error-runtime overview.

- Document tool payload helper contracts for plaintext tool-call blocks, parser options, stripped reply text, carrier fields, and structured payload precedence.

- Document keyed async queue contracts for hook timing, per-key tail maps, rejection-safe bookkeeping, newest-tail cleanup, and class wrapper behavior.

- Document webhook request guard contracts for body-read profiles, in-flight limits, method/rate/content-type guards, lifecycle release, and body-reader response hooks.

- Document runtime store contracts for shared plugin-id slots, duplicate module imports, strict/nullable runtime accessors, and teardown/test clearing.

- Document Telegram account compatibility facade contracts for resolved account fields, token source semantics, merged config ownership, and lazy facade loading.

- Document tool-send extraction fallback behavior for optional account and thread fields when the model supplies only a target.

- Document plugin command runner contracts for result/options fields, empty argv handling, timeout stderr synthesis, and process-runner abstraction boundaries.

- Document OpenAI/Codex auth helper contracts for identity fields, non-throwing JWT payload decoding, and opaque-subject profile-name fallbacks.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test packages/markdown-core/src/render-aware-chunking.test.ts packages/markdown-core/src/tables.test.ts packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-code.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: markdown render docs for marker/link/options contracts and close-stack ordering comments; markdown code-span docs for inline scanner state, fenced-code precedence, and unfinished-span chunk protection; markdown IR docs for style/link/table contracts, parse options, slicing, and table-aware parse metadata; markdown table/chunk docs for span rebasing, table mode, link preservation, and block-mode conversion behavior; shared JSON/schema helper docs for balanced fragment offsets and provider schema keyword stripping shape preservation; assistant error formatter docs for provider payload/status/html/internal-error parsing and bounded UI copy; shared utility docs for lazy loader cache/retry behavior, message content block reference semantics, and regexp literal escaping; shared runtime/policy docs for lazy runtime proxies, Windows-safe import specifiers, and reserved gateway method scope coercion; node matching docs for candidate identity fields and blank-query default picker behavior; device auth docs for entry/store fields and adapter read/write contracts; final-tag parser docs for match offsets/flags and quoted-attribute scanning; custom command config docs for slash command normalization, description trimming, validation issues, and accepted-command duplicate tracking; scoped expiring id cache docs for record/has/clear method behavior; store writer queue docs for task promise hooks and per-path FIFO drain state; requirements docs for resource categories, metadata inputs, config diagnostics, and remote capability hooks; config-eval docs for RuntimeRequires binary/env/config eligibility fields; thread binding lifecycle docs for binding/activity timestamps and idle/max-age override semantics; node list docs for effective node, pending pairing, paired node, and pairing list payload fields; usage payload docs for session usage entries, aggregate groups, and gateway usage result fields; usage timeseries docs for bucket values and cumulative fields; entry status docs for presentation metadata, requirement diagnostics, and eligibility result fields; mention gating docs for inbound facts, channel policy, implicit mention kinds, and decision fields; command gating docs for authorizer state, access-groups-off fallback mode, and control-command gate fields; thread binding policy docs for spawn kind/context and resolved policy fields; channel target/policy docs for parser order, normalized keys, provider-native ids, and deny-over-allow mention-pattern precedence; ack reaction docs for gate facts, WhatsApp adapter mapping, send-handle lifecycle, and post-reply cleanup; direct-DM guard docs for accepted kinds, timestamp skew, byte caps, and rate limits; route projection docs for delivery-context conversion, conversation/binding projection, native command session target resolution, and unscoped-account matching; conversation resolution docs for binding placement lookup, command/inbound precedence, fallback ordering, and provider stop-on-null semantics; inbound event context docs for builder params/results, supplemental visibility filters, command-turn derivation, and self-quote media suppression; channel turn helper docs for reply pipeline params, history window methods, and bot-loop guard facts; message receipt docs for source/part/receipt fields, durable requirement triggers, durable send intent fields, and receipt id ordering; live message docs for preview draft/finalizer adapter fields, capability proof results, capability listing/proof helpers, and fallback cleanup; durable message docs for batch params/outcomes, send context preview state, recovery records, receive ack context, and original-error preservation during cleanup; message outbound bridge docs for legacy result/adapter fields, rendered batch plan items, receipt id precedence, poll kind normalization, and rich payload kind inference; durable ingress docs for queue/journal method contracts, claim-token guards, race cleanup, tombstones, and bounded pruning; message receive/capability docs for ack method contracts, stage ownership, media URL filtering, and durable final option semantics; inbound reply bridge docs for compatibility aliases, record/dispatch params, durable fallback ownership, and stale header removal; channel turn delivery docs for delivery fields, adapter hooks, durable fallback, thread override semantics, and unknown-send queue policy; channel turn adapter docs for record/history options, assembled/prepared turn fields, lifecycle log fields, adapter methods, and runner params; channel config helper docs for write helpers, accessor fallback, top-level section shape, deletion behavior, and hybrid default-account semantics; channel snapshot/presence docs for credential projection, URL redaction, configured-signal discovery, and persisted-auth metadata caching; channel match/allowlist docs for match metadata, fallback precedence, nested allowlist decisions, wildcard behavior, and ordered candidate matching; direct-DM allow-from docs for access-group parsing, DM/group allowlist merging, sender checks, legacy runtime fields, and optional pairing challenge ownership; inbound debounce/stream docs for debounce bypass rules, resolved debouncer state, stream loop methods, background error ownership, and false-result retry semantics; reply prefix/typing guard docs for prefix context/options, route params, non-throwing error behavior, and tripped guard skip semantics; thread binding/conversation label docs for timeout labels, thread names, intro/farewell params, cwd detail ordering, and id disambiguation; channel diagnostics/session docs for sender identity validation, logging helper params, lazy session runtime loading, and best-effort metadata writes; webhook guard/account docs for integer option normalization, in-memory limiter/counter/anomaly APIs, bounded Map LRU touch semantics, idempotent release, and configured-account id dedupe; session/silent-reply docs for gateway identity/model/runtime/list/patch payload fields, policy types/defaults, webchat classification, and direct-chat audible guard; extension-shared docs for passive status/traffic summaries, passive monitor lifecycle, logger-backed runtime fallback, open-DM allowFrom validation, status issue helpers, deferred promises, plugin config issue mapping, read-only env secret checks, package version lookup, and ambient proxy/TLS resolution; status-helper docs for runtime lifecycle/account status adapter fields, computed account status inputs/results, config-issue account fields, dependent credential issue collector options, and optional runtime transport field emission; migration helper docs for item status helpers, summaries, config patch details/path reads/merges/writes/conflict checks, patch/manual item factories, patch apply race recheck, manual apply, redaction helpers, and stale file-top overview removal; QA live transport scenario docs for standard ids, scenario fields, baseline set, requested selection, coverage collection, duplicate ordering, and missing-standard detection; browser/config runtime docs for browser executable fields, browser host-inspection facade cache and wrappers, runtime config requirement, plugin config object resolution, scalar config rejection, and live/startup config fallback; provider model shared docs for provider id normalization, provider-prefixed owner hints, preview model id normalization, replay family semantics/options, captured replay family policy, and stale file-top overview removal; command status runtime docs for direct status params, synthetic /status reply helper contract, session-key canonicalization note, channel activation callback, and unauthorized reasoning-state privacy guard; assistant text segment docs for text signature/extraction option fields, explicit-phase block isolation, optional text concatenation params, join-segment options, and trim output semantics.; fetch auth/OAuth/chunking docs for scoped bearer retry params, original-response preservation, OAuth string-only encoding, PKCE verifier alphabets, and shared chunk resolver fallback ownership.; account fallback/optional setup docs for explicit-account no-redirect behavior, plugin install guidance, no-core-config-mutation adapter behavior, and fail-loud wizard finalization.; persistent dedupe docs for TTL/cache/file options, namespace/time/error overrides, warmup/check APIs, claim result states, and commit/release waiter propagation.; provider tool compatibility docs for unsupported-keyword diagnostics, Gemini/OpenAI/DeepSeek normalization and inspection helpers, OpenAI strict-schema violation paths, and compat family hook selection.; allowlist config edit docs for path metadata, group/name resolution result types, legacy DM path resolution, prototype-safe account write targets, compact default-account storage, normalized removal matching, and legacy cleanup after canonical writes.; provider onboarding docs for preset applier modes, alias preservation, normalized agent/provider config writes, primary model handling, default/catalog model merges, model allowlist insertion, and stale file-top note removal.; provider catalog shared docs for configured entry projection, live catalog cache key/TTL behavior, manifest catalog conversion, configured model reads, native streaming usage support checks, compat application, and file-top note removal.; approval reaction runtime docs for target store methods, decision/target/payload content types, stable binding order, emoji normalization, target resolution, prompt/fallback builders, bounded persistent target storage, and file-top compatibility block removal.; safe record docs for hostile/proxy-safe record detection, safe property reads, array entry copying with throwing-index skips, and record-valued entry copying.; browser maintenance docs for session-key cleanup params, lazy browser plugin maintenance facade loading, zero-on-unavailable behavior, and best-effort warning behavior during session lifecycle cleanup., plugin SDK runtime helper maintainability; no runtime behavior intended, tool payload helper maintainability; no runtime behavior intended, keyed async queue helper maintainability; no runtime behavior intended, webhook request guard helper maintainability; no runtime behavior intended, runtime store helper maintainability; no runtime behavior intended, Telegram account facade maintainability; no runtime behavior intended, tool-send fallback maintainability; target.title: feat: pass trusted sender metadata via systemInputProvenance for operator clients
target.body:
## Summary
When operator-authenticated gateway clients send chat messages via `chat.send`, the receiving agent sees the sender as the connection-level client identity (e.g. "gateway-client") even when richer per-message provenance is available. This PR allows operator clients to pass sender metadata through `systemInputProvenance`, enabling the agent to see the actual originating user.

## Changes
- **primitives.ts**: Add `senderId`, `senderName`, `senderRole` optional fields to `InputProvenanceSchema`
- **chat.ts**: When `canInjectSystemProvenance(client)` is true and the provenance has sender fields, use them for `SenderId` / `SenderName` / `SenderUsername` instead of `clientInfo`. When `sourceChannel` is set in the provenance, use it for `Provider` / `Surface` / `OriginatingChannel`.

## Safety
- All changes are gated behind `canInjectSystemProvenance(client)` — only clients with `operator.admin` scope are affected
- Falls back to existing `clientInfo`-based defaults when sender fields are absent or caller lacks operator scope
- No breaking change: non-operator clients and clients without sender fields are completely unaffected
- `additionalProperties: false` is maintained on the schema

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Operator-authenticated clients can now pass trusted sender metadata (senderId, senderName) through systemInputProvenance. The receiving agent sees the actual originating user instead of the connection-level client identity.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw gateway built from this PR branch, running on localhost:18790. Connected via WebSocket operator client with operator.admin scope.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```
node test.mjs <operator-token>
```
Script connects as operator, sends chat.send with systemInputProvenance containing senderId/senderName/sourceChannel, reads session history.

**Evidence after fix:** Gateway log output:
```
[gateway] senderId=u-123 senderName=Test User sourceChannel=logicos:test clientInfoId=gateway-client
```
Terminal output:
```
=== Sender Metadata Test ===
Connect: OK
Auth: OK (operator.admin scope)
chat.send: OK
✅ SUCCESS: Trusted sender metadata correctly applied
- senderId "u-123" used instead of clientInfo.id "gateway-client"
- senderName "Test User" used instead of clientInfo.displayName
- sourceChannel "logicos:test" available for Provider/Surface/OriginatingChannel
```

**Observed result after fix:** MsgContext receives senderId and senderName from systemInputProvenance when sent by an operator-authenticated client. Falls back to clientInfo values when sender fields are absent. Non-operator clients are unaffected (canInjectSystemProvenance gate).

**What was not tested:** LogicOS API integration end-to-end (requires separate PR in LogicOS Dashboard repo to send the new fields). The gateway-level passthrough is fully verified.

target.title: fix(cron): bootstrap outbound channel targets
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #77712 by allowing outbound target resolution to bootstrap configured channel plugins before returning unsupported-channel errors.
- Keeps cron delivery previews aligned with runtime delivery for configured bundled channels like Feishu.
- Adds regression coverage that target resolution requests plugin bootstrap.

## Background
Cron list delivery preview could show `Unsupported channel: feishu` even when Feishu was configured and recent deliveries succeeded. The preview path resolves outbound targets before the channel plugin has necessarily been loaded.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/outbound/targets.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: configured Feishu outbound target resolution bootstraps the channel plugin instead of reporting `Unsupported channel: feishu`.
- Real environment tested: macOS 25.3.0, Node v24.13.1, real local OpenClaw checkout `/tmp/openclaw-pr-77709`, active user OpenClaw config present, active plugin registry explicitly cleared before the call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the same outbound target resolver used by cron delivery preview with a redacted configured `channels.feishu` block and a Feishu `ou_...` target.
- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ pnpm exec tsx -e "import { setActivePluginRegistry } from './src/plugins/runtime.ts'; import { resolveOutboundTarget } from './src/infra/outbound/targets.ts'; setActivePluginRegistry(undefined as any); const cfg={channels:{feishu:{enabled:true,appId:'cli_xxx',appSecret:'redacted'}}}; const res=resolveOutboundTarget({channel:'feishu',to:'ou_3e605fadb6dc02335602d16ec60bc8c3',cfg:cfg as any,mode:'explicit'}); console.log(JSON.stringify(res,null,2));"
{
  "ok": true,
  "to": "ou_3e605fadb6dc02335602d16ec60bc8c3"
}
[plugins] plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: feishu-openclaw-plugin (.../.openclaw/extensions/feishu-openclaw-plugin/index.js). Set plugins.allow to explicit trusted ids.
[plugins] 1 plugin(s) failed to initialize (validation: feishu-openclaw-plugin). Run 'openclaw plugins list' for details.
```

- Observed result after fix: the configured bundled Feishu channel resolved successfully with `{ "ok": true }` instead of returning `Unsupported channel: feishu`, even before a channel plugin registry was preloaded.
- What was not tested: live Feishu message send; this change is limited to outbound target resolution/cron preview and does not alter message transport.

## Impact
Configured outbound channels can now be lazily loaded for preview/target resolution, reducing false unsupported-channel warnings without changing target normalization semantics.

target.body:
﻿## Summary
- preserve `stuck_recovery` through reply-run session-id fallback and embedded-run cancel plumbing so recovery no longer degrades into a fake user abort
- require stale tracked progress before active embedded/reply work is abort-drained, even when active-abort recovery is enabled
- add regressions for fresh terminal progress, fresh active reply work, and streamed model progress so long-but-active runs stay alive

## Testing

## Notes
- `diagnostics.stuckSessionWarnMs` / `diagnostics.stuckSessionAbortMs` and the default `warnMs x 3` / minimum 5 minute behavior are already documented in the current docs/config help, so this PR focuses on the runtime regressions from `#88870`.
- I did not rerun a live Feishu or `claude-cli` repro in this environment.

Closes #88870

target.title: fix(google): use parameters not parametersJsonSchema in realtime FunctionDeclarations
target.body:
## Summary

Switch the Gemini Live realtime voice provider's `FunctionDeclaration` from `parametersJsonSchema` to `parameters` so the preview audio models actually honor tool parameter schemas.

## The bug

The Live API preview audio models (`gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-*`) silently ignore `parametersJsonSchema` — they read only the function `description` and invent parameter names like `query`, `prompt`, `description`, etc. Every tool call then fails validation against the tool's real schema and the model retries with another invented name in a tight loop. With `openclaw_agent_consult` enabled (e.g. for voice agents that need CRM/memory access mid-call), this manifests as the agent going silent after asking a tool-needing question.

Per Google's [FunctionDeclaration spec](https://ai.google.dev/api/caching#FunctionDeclaration), `parameters` (Schema, OpenAPI 3.0.3) and `parametersJsonSchema` (raw JSON Schema) are **mutually exclusive**. Both are valid, but `parameters` is the older, universally-honored field, while `parametersJsonSchema` is honored only by some newer models. Our `RealtimeVoiceTool.parameters` shape (`type: "object"` with `properties`/`required`) is already a valid OpenAPI 3.0.3 Schema subset, so it works in both — only `parameters` is honored everywhere.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts` — 16/16 pass (existing connect-config assertion updated to expect `parameters`, providing regression coverage)
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — file is clean (other extensions/core errors are pre-existing on `main` and unrelated: `extensions/discord` carbon, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner` websocket-cached transport, `src/media/qr-runtime` qrcode-tui)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on touched files — clean

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: With `parametersJsonSchema`, Gemini Live's preview audio models silently ignore the tool parameter schema and invent random parameter names on every `openclaw_agent_consult` tool call, causing every consult to instant-fail with `{"error":"question required"}` and the live phone agent to go silent on any tool-needing question.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw gateway 2026.5.7 running locally on Linux 6.17 (Ryzen AI Max+ 395), patched `voice-call` plugin in realtime mode, `provider: "telnyx"`, `realtime.provider: "google"` → Gemini Live model `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-12-2025`, real Telnyx number `+1 (651) 422-0083` placing/receiving real PSTN calls from a real US cell phone. ngrok tunnel `stretch-unclaimed-foe.ngrok-free.dev` for the realtime WebSocket and webhook surface.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Restart gateway with patched `extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.ts` so `buildFunctionDeclarations` emits `parameters` instead of `parametersJsonSchema`: `openclaw gateway restart`
2. Place a live inbound call to the Telnyx number from a real cell phone
3. Ask a tool-needing question: "Look up prospect Laura Dunbar", "How many prospects are currently in the system?", "Search for Dennis Batty", "Who is Ryan Boog?"
4. Tail the gateway log: `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway -f | grep "realtime tool"`

**Evidence after fix**: Redacted runtime logs from the gateway during the live calls — `[voice-call] realtime tool call` / `realtime tool result` lines from `extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts:executeToolCall` (the patched logging surface). Log lines below are copied verbatim from `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway`:

Before this patch (sent as `parametersJsonSchema`) — the model invents field names and every consult instant-fails in 8–28 ms with `error=question required`:

```
[voice-call] realtime tool call: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=3c034b84-c776-43b7-8bc6-fe6bd9f4cd2f args={"query":"Look up prospect Laura Dunbar"}
[voice-call] realtime tool result: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=3c034b84-c776-43b7-8bc6-fe6bd9f4cd2f ms=11 result={"error":"question required"}
[voice-call] realtime tool call: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=9a10234f-4d07-4a33-9862-aef5f5cf7d60 args={"description":"Search for prospect named Laura Dunbar."}
[voice-call] realtime tool result: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=9a10234f-4d07-4a33-9862-aef5f5cf7d60 ms=28 result={"error":"question required"}
```

After this patch (sent as `parameters`) — the model honors the schema, uses the declared `question` field, and consults complete in 3.5–9 s with real openclaw agent answers:

```
[voice-call] realtime tool call: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=ac17e818-6054-4237-8565-387deabed891 args={"question":"How many prospects are currently in the system?"}
[voice-call] realtime tool result: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=ac17e818-6054-4237-8565-387deabed891 ms=4493 result={"text":"I currently have 67 prospects in the system, though one is a test entry and should be ignored."}
[voice-call] realtime tool call: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=ac17e818-6054-4237-8565-387deabed891 args={"question":"Search for prospect with the name 'Dennis Batty' (B-A-T-T-Y)."}
[voice-call] realtime tool result: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=ac17e818-6054-4237-8565-387deabed891 ms=3472 result={"text":"Yes, I found Dennis Batty of Dennis Batty & Associates. They are listed under Architecture."}
[voice-call] realtime tool call: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=ac17e818-6054-4237-8565-387deabed891 args={"question":"What information is available about Ryan Boog?"}
[voice-call] realtime tool result: name=openclaw_agent_consult callId=ac17e818-6054-4237-8565-387deabed891 ms=5094 result={"text":"Ryan Boog is the owner of Happy Dog Web Productions and Hoist..."}
```

**Observed result after fix**: Live phone caller hears Rosellen ask the question, hears the working-on-it filler immediately, and hears a real CRM-backed answer 3.5–9 seconds later. The model uses the schema's declared `question` field on every consult call instead of `query`/`description`/`prompt`. Voice turn latency on chitchat (no consult) remains sub-second. No regressions on the Twilio realtime path during the same session.

**What was not tested**: Non-preview Gemini Live model variants (only the preview `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-12-2025` was tested — `parameters` works for it; `parametersJsonSchema` was the silent-ignore path); OpenAI Realtime path (uses a separate provider plugin not touched by this PR).

## Sources

- [Google FunctionDeclaration spec](https://ai.google.dev/api/caching#FunctionDeclaration) — `parametersJsonSchema` is documented as "mutually exclusive with parameters".

target.title: fix(skills): warn for untrusted local sources
target.body:
Fixes #57535

## Problem summary
Local skills from workspace, `.agents`, managed, and extra skill roots were shown as ordinary ready/blocked skills. Operators had no first-class status signal distinguishing ClawHub/bundled skills from local skill files that should be reviewed before enablement or invocation.

## Root cause
The skills loader already tracks each skill source, and ClawHub installs already write `.clawhub/origin.json`, but `buildWorkspaceSkillStatus` collapsed that into `source`/`bundled` only. CLI and Control UI status surfaces therefore had no trust metadata to render.

## Architectural reasoning
This keeps runtime loading semantics unchanged and adds trust classification at the status/reporting layer. Built-in skills are trusted as `openclaw-bundled`; valid ClawHub origin metadata is trusted as `clawhub`; reviewed local roots configured in `skills.load.trustedDirs` are trusted as `trusted-dir`; known local sources without either signal are marked `untrustedLocalSource` with a warning. The change is additive to the status API and avoids coupling core loader behavior to owner-specific skill ids.

## Testing performed
- `pnpm test src/agents/skills-status.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm test src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/skills-status.ts src/agents/skills-status.test.ts src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.format.ts src/cli/skills-cli.test.ts src/config/types.skills.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts ui/src/ui/types.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills-shared.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts docs/tools/skills.md docs/tools/skills-config.md` passed.
- `pnpm lint:core` passed.
- `pnpm lint:docs` passed.
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx` passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed via a temporary `P:\` mapping to avoid the repo path-with-spaces issue.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed via the same temporary mapping.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` passed via the same temporary mapping.
- `pnpm config:docs:check` initially detected expected config baseline drift; after `pnpm config:docs:gen`, `pnpm config:docs:check` passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed after clean rebase.
- `pnpm check:changed` passed locally. Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable because `blacksmith` is not installed on PATH.

`pnpm test:changed` was also run locally and selected a broad `unit-fast` shard; it failed in 19 unrelated files with existing Windows/path/host assumptions such as `/tmp` becoming `C:\tmp`, POSIX table glyph expectations on Windows output, A2UI scaffold 404 in `extensions/canvas`, and temp SQLite cleanup `EBUSY`. The focused touched-surface tests above passed.

`pnpm format:docs:check` could not be used as proof in this environment because the wrapper crashed on Node v22.15.0 with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')` in `scripts/format-docs.mjs`; targeted `oxfmt --check`, `lint:docs`, and `docs:check-mdx` passed.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: A temporary workspace skill without ClawHub origin metadata is now classified as an untrusted local source.
- Real environment tested: Windows checkout on branch `hemant/57535-untrusted-skill-sources`, Node v22.15.0, pnpm 10.33.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `pnpm exec tsx` script from the repo root that created a temporary `<workspace>/skills/local-review/SKILL.md`, called `buildWorkspaceSkillStatus(workspace)`, and rendered `formatSkillsCheck(report, {})`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local run:

```json
{
  "name": "local-review",
  "source": "openclaw-workspace",
  "trustSource": "local",
  "untrustedLocalSource": true,
  "hasWarning": true
}
```

```text
Untrusted local sources
local-review
Add the directory to skills.load.trustedDirs only if you trust this workspace-owned skill source.
```

- Observed result after fix: Local skill loading still succeeded and the status/check surface warned before users enable or invoke the local skill.
- What was not tested: No browser screenshot was captured because this is a status/API/CLI/UI-rendering change covered by jsdom UI tests rather than a visual layout change.

## Risk analysis
Low-to-medium. The classification is additive and synchronous, using small local origin/trusted-dir checks during status generation. It does not alter skill discovery, precedence, eligibility, model visibility, command visibility, or execution. Main residual risk is users seeing new warnings for legitimate local/plugin-provided skill roots until they add reviewed directories to `skills.load.trustedDirs`.

## Backward compatibility
Backward-compatible. Existing skill config continues to load the same skills. New `SkillStatusEntry` fields are additive, and `skills.load.trustedDirs` is optional. Existing `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets` behavior is unchanged; docs now clarify that it controls loader containment while `trustedDirs` controls warning suppression.

## Screenshots/logs
No screenshots. Relevant command output is summarized above.

target.title: fix(agents): emit reasoning stream events to gateway clients without channel callback (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Gateway-connected clients (webchat, custom WebSocket clients) never receive live `agent stream=thinking` events during agent runs, even when the user has set `reasoningLevel: "stream"`. Thinking only surfaces post-run via `chat.history`.
- **Why it matters:** Anyone building a non-channel WS client (webchat, mobile apps over the gateway) can't render reasoning tokens live. The plumbing exists end-to-end on both sides — gateway broadcasts `agent stream=thinking`, `chat.history` returns thinking blocks — but a single guard short-circuits emission for the gateway path.
- **What changed:** Two edits in `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts`. (1) Decouple `streamReasoning` from `onReasoningStream`. Keep `canShowReasoning` so the visibility gate still applies. (2) Make `params.onReasoningStream(...)` invocation null-safe with `?.()` so existing channel callers (Telegram, Mattermost) keep working.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No protocol change. No UI rendering changes. Only the chat.send → auto-reply path was tested live — entry points like cron and ACP go through `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts` and were not exercised.

> AI-assisted: yes (Claude Code). Live behavior verified by the author on a real WS client.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Addresses #42261, #51505, #48995 — all describe the same `streamReasoning` callback-requirement bug fixed here. (Already closed without a fix landing; flagging as cross-references.)
- Partially addresses #5086 — this PR enables gateway emission of `stream: "thinking"` events. The webchat UI rendering side is a follow-up.
- Related #47613 — open PR with the same root cause. This PR keeps `canShowReasoning` (codex review concern on #47613) and adds the no-callback regression test the review asked for.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** With `reasoningLevel: "stream"` set, gateway WS clients receive zero `agent stream=thinking` events during a chat.send-originated agent run.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (this branch installed at `~/.nvm/.../node_modules/openclaw/`), Linux 6.17 / Node 24.13. WS client: a custom Android client that decodes `agent stream=thinking` events and renders matching `type=thinking` content blocks. Inference: GitHub Copilot, GPT 5.4.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `openclaw config set agents.defaults.reasoningDefault stream`
  2. Restart gateway
  3. Open the WS client, send a prompt that elicits reasoning
- **Evidence after fix:** Screen recording attached below — thinking content streams live during the turn rather than only appearing after `chat.history` reload.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d206c652-7d84-43e0-9c3c-744b20d9f0d9



- **Observed result after fix:** Live `agent` events with `stream: "thinking"` and `data: { text, delta }` arrive on the WS during the turn.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Providers other than GitHub Copilot / GPT 5.4.
  - Entry points other than chat.send → auto-reply (cron, ACP, etc.). Those go through `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts` and I did not exercise them; behavior there may or may not be equivalent.
  - Channel paths (Telegram preview, Mattermost draft) — only covered by the existing test suite, not live.
  - Control UI rendering of the new event stream (separate follow-up).
  - Multi-agent / subagent runs.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `streamReasoning` in `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts:139` required **both** `reasoningMode === "stream"` AND `typeof params.onReasoningStream === "function"` to flip true. `chat.send` (`src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`) doesn't supply that callback in `replyOptions`, so the flag stayed false for chat.send-originated runs and `emitReasoningStream` short-circuited before the global `emitAgentEvent({ stream: "thinking", ... })` could fire.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No no-callback test coverage for `streamReasoning`. Existing reasoning-stream tests at `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.subscribeembeddedpisession.test.ts:184,718,754` all instantiate `onReasoningStream`.
- **Contributing context:** The callback dependency made sense when reasoning streaming was Telegram-preview-only. Adding `emitAgentEvent({ stream: "thinking" })` for gateway clients made the callback no longer the only sink, but the guard wasn't loosened.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:**
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file:** `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.subscribeembeddedpisession.test.ts` — new `describe` block with 2 tests.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:**
  1. `reasoningMode: "stream"` with no `onReasoningStream` callback → `agent stream=thinking` events fire on the global event bus.
  2. `reasoningMode: "stream"` with `thinkingLevel: "off"` → no thinking events (visibility gate honored — `canShowReasoning` preserved).
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Pinning the no-callback path catches any refactor that re-introduces a callback dependency. The visibility-gate test pins the `canShowReasoning` invariant separately so a naive simplification can't broaden reasoning exposure.
- **Existing test that already covers this:** None — existing tests at lines 184, 718, 754 all supply `onReasoningStream`.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When `reasoningLevel: "stream"` is set (via `agents.defaults.reasoningDefault: "stream"` or `/reasoning stream`):

- WS clients subscribed to `agent` events now receive `stream: "thinking"` events with `data: { text, delta }` during agent turns.
- No behavior change for `reasoningMode: "off"` or `"on"`, or for any session where `thinkingLevel: "off"` is set.
- No behavior change for channel paths that already supply `onReasoningStream`.

## Diagram

`N/A`

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No` — the `agent stream=thinking` broadcast already exists on the same code path; this PR stops short-circuiting before it fires. Visibility gating (`canShowReasoning` from `thinkingLevel !== "off"`) preserved.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.17
- Runtime: Node 24.13.1, gateway as systemd user service
- Model/provider: GitHub Copilot, GPT 5.4
- Integration/channel: Custom WS client over `chat.send` / `chat.history`
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.reasoningDefault: "stream"`

### Steps

1. Build & install this branch into the gateway location.
2. `openclaw config set agents.defaults.reasoningDefault stream`
3. Restart the gateway.
4. Connect a WS client that handles `agent stream=thinking` events.
5. Send a prompt that triggers reasoning.

### Expected

`agent` events with `stream: "thinking"`, `data: { text, delta }` arrive during the run. `text` is cumulative; `delta` is the new text since the last event.

### Actual

Matches expected. See attached recording.

## Evidence

- [x] New regression tests pass
- [x] Screen recording (attached above)

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Live thinking streams on a custom WS client with `reasoningLevel: "stream"` and GitHub Copilot / GPT 5.4 — recording attached.
  - The two new regression tests pass; the modified test file is 39/39.
- **What I did not verify:**
  - Other providers, other reasoning-capable models.
  - Entry points other than chat.send → auto-reply (cron, ACP, etc.).
  - Channel paths (Telegram / Mattermost) live — only via existing tests.
  - Control UI rendering of the new stream.
  - Multi-agent / subagent runs.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I will reply to or resolve every bot review conversation I address.
- [x] I will leave unresolved only the conversations that still need maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** A user with `reasoningMode: "stream"` and `thinkingLevel: "off"` would, with the original PR #47613 patch, start seeing live reasoning broadcast — broader than intended.
  - **Mitigation:** This PR keeps `canShowReasoning`. Test 2 in the new suite pins this invariant.
- **Risk:** A future refactor re-introduces the callback dependency, silently regressing chat.send / WS clients.
  - **Mitigation:** Test 1 asserts `agent stream=thinking` events fire when `onReasoningStream` is absent, against the global event bus.

target.title: feat(ui): autostart hidden chat bootstrap
target.body:
## Summary
- parse one-shot `autostart` from Control UI/WebChat URLs and strip it after capture
- defer captured autostart requests while a gateway URL confirmation is pending
- run the bootstrap prompt only after an empty `chat.history` load succeeds
- send autostart through the chat controller as a hidden, no-local-echo prompt with stable idempotency

## Dependency
Draft until #79652 lands. This UI path relies on the `chat.send.hideUserMessage` Gateway protocol flag added there.

## Why
This keeps the URL/autostart behavior separate from the Gateway protocol change so reviewers can inspect the browser flow without also reviewing the server-side hidden-send authorization path.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Control UI/WebChat deep links can stage a one-shot bootstrap prompt that becomes a hidden `chat.send` request after an empty `chat.history` load, without leaving the triggering bootstrap prompt in chat history.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS source checkout using the split UI branch with the dependent Gateway behavior from #79652, foreground OpenClaw Gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:19886` with token auth and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Verified the UI URL capture/autostart dispatch path with the targeted browser-controller tests below, then ran the resulting hidden Gateway request shape against a real local Gateway: `chat.history`, `chat.send` with `{ "sessionKey": "main", "message": "/context list", "hideUserMessage": true }`, and `chat.history` again.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live local gateway client run of the hidden request emitted by the UI autostart path:

```json
{
  "connected": true,
  "url": "ws://127.0.0.1:19886",
  "before": {
    "sessionId": null,
    "messageCount": 0,
    "texts": []
  },
  "send": {
    "runId": "proof-hidden-send-1778301902610",
    "status": "started"
  },
  "after": {
    "sessionId": "933b48d9-d050-4337-b09f-291cd535db91",
    "messageCount": 0,
    "texts": [],
    "containsHiddenPrompt": false
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The real gateway accepted the hidden send shape used by autostart, returned a backing `sessionId`, and did not expose the hidden bootstrap trigger in chat history. The UI tests verify URL stripping, pending-gateway deferral, empty-history gating, stable idempotency, and no local user echo.
- **What was not tested**: Full manual browser screenshot of the URL flow; this PR remains draft until the dependent Gateway PR #79652 lands.

## Testing
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts -t "omits hideUserMessage for normal chat sends|can send a hidden prompt without local echoing the user turn|reuses a caller-provided idempotency key|does not inject an orphaned assistant error|captures and strips autostart prompts from the URL|autostarts a hidden prompt once for an empty chat session|skips autostart when chat history failed to load|preserves autostart when the hidden send fails|clears session binding after a successful hidden send"`

target.title: chore: harden OpenClaw certification gates
target.body:
## Summary
- harden certification gates and supply-chain fixtures
- add reviewed Gitleaks/Trivy policy coverage
- normalize SBOM ownership coverage for current dependency surface
- strengthen regression tests around notification/session behavior

## Local proof on rebased upstream main
- base: openclaw/openclaw@7e26b59f
- head: e22909746ab28956e8c4b42e94685e0fff8e033b
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile: PASS
- pnpm deps:sbom-risk:check: PASS
- git diff --check: PASS
- pnpm check: PASS
- pnpm build: PASS
- pnpm test: PASS, 76 Vitest shards

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Certification/supply-chain hardening changes remain compatible with a real OpenClaw runtime and do not break the live MBP gateway after the patch was rebased onto current upstream main.
- **Real environment tested**: Halvos-MacBook-Pro, macOS 26.3 arm64, OpenClaw 2026.5.7, Node 24.15.0, gateway on loopback http://127.0.0.1:18790, branch cert/s-plus-plus-signoff-20260508-212449 at e22909746ab28956e8c4b42e94685e0fff8e033b.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Terminal capture from the real setup:

```text
cd /Users/halvo/onboarding-ashley/openclaw-certification-20260508-212449
git fetch origin main --prune
git rebase origin/main
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm deps:sbom-risk:check
git diff --check
pnpm check
pnpm build
pnpm test
openclaw config validate
launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/ai.openclaw.gateway
openclaw status --all
awk '$0 >= "2026-05-08T22:59:44"' /Users/halvo/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log | rg 'failed to start server|MCP server connection timed out' || true
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted live output from the real MBP runtime:

```text
Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Gateway: local · ws://127.0.0.1:18790 (local loopback) · reachable 24ms · auth token · Halvos-MacBook-Pro.local app 2026.5.7 macos 26.3

Secrets: none
Gateway restart log excerpt:
2026-05-08T22:59:44.489+10:00 [shutdown] started: gateway stopping
2026-05-08T22:59:44.520+10:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 31ms
2026-05-08T22:59:45.013+10:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-08T22:59:46.942+10:00 [gateway] http server listening (15 plugins: active-memory, anthropic, bonjour, browser, device-pair, file-transfer, llm-task, memory-core, memory-wiki, openshell, phone-control, skill-workshop, talk-voice, thread-ownership, webhooks; 1.6s)

Context7 direct MCP inspector proof: tools/list returned resolve-library-id and query-docs.
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The real gateway restarted cleanly on loopback, stayed reachable, config validation passed, no fresh MCP startup failures appeared after the restart marker, Context7 exposed its expected tools through the official MCP inspector, and the rebased code branch passed install, SBOM ownership, whitespace, check, build, and full test suite locally.
- **What was not tested**: Full 24h soak, provider-side secret rotation, MDM/EDR enrollment, and GitHub alert APIs requiring elevated upstream admin/security access.

## Notes
- PR is opened from a fork because halvo78 has no direct push permission to openclaw/openclaw.
- Commit author/committer uses GitHub noreply email to satisfy email privacy protection.

target.title: feat(gateway): support hidden chat sends
target.body:
## Summary
- add `chat.send.hideUserMessage` to the Gateway protocol schema and Swift protocol model
- require `operator.admin` before a hidden send can suppress the triggering user turn
- reject hidden sends with `deliver: true` or attachments so the hidden path stays narrow
- keep assistant output/history behavior intact while skipping only the user transcript write

## Why
This is the small protocol/Gateway foundation for hidden bootstrap turns. It does not add URL autostart behavior; that is intentionally split into follow-up #79653.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `chat.send` accepts `hideUserMessage: true` for admin-scoped callers, does not record the triggering user prompt in chat history, and rejects attachments on the hidden path.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS source checkout at `a1884362174eaf72cae7b2ddcbd2430c7067a736`, foreground OpenClaw Gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:19886` with token auth and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started a real gateway with `pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --port 19886 --bind loopback --auth token --token <redacted> --ws-log compact`, connected over the real Gateway WebSocket with admin/read/write operator scopes, called `chat.history`, called `chat.send` with `{ "sessionKey": "main", "message": "/context list", "hideUserMessage": true }`, called `chat.history` again, then attempted a hidden send with an image attachment.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live local gateway client run:

```json
{
  "connected": true,
  "url": "ws://127.0.0.1:19886",
  "before": {
    "sessionId": null,
    "messageCount": 0,
    "texts": []
  },
  "send": {
    "runId": "proof-hidden-send-1778301902610",
    "status": "started"
  },
  "after": {
    "sessionId": "933b48d9-d050-4337-b09f-291cd535db91",
    "messageCount": 0,
    "texts": [],
    "containsHiddenPrompt": false
  },
  "attachmentGuard": {
    "ok": false,
    "message": "hideUserMessage cannot be combined with attachments"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The real gateway accepted the hidden send, returned a backing `sessionId` on the next history load, did not expose `/context list` in chat history, and rejected hidden sends with attachments.
- **What was not tested**: Browser URL autostart; that is split into dependent follow-up #79653.

## Testing
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send can hide the triggering user turn from chat history|chat.send requires operator.admin to hide the triggering user turn|chat.send rejects hideUserMessage with attachments"`
